adaptations: tools=claude only: .claude/agents + .claude/workflows installed; cursor/codex/copilot adapters and the .project-factory/ vendored docs intentionally omitted

git hooks copied to .githooks/ + scripts/hooks-*.mjs but core.hooksPath deliberately NOT set: the repo's 151 commits carry no Refs:/Slice: trailers, so commit-msg would reject every commit touching src/. Arm with 'git config core.hooksPath .githooks' once the trailer convention is adopted
no verify-commit made at G0: repo had uncommitted export-share work on main; init step 4's 'git commit --allow-empty' skipped

CI trimmed from templates/ci/github-actions.yml: test:integration, test:e2e, playwright install, test:coverage, DATABASE_URL/BETTER_AUTH_* env and 'npm audit --audit-level=high' removed - no mechanism exists (no DB, no Playwright, no coverage provider). Added capability:check + factory-integrity instead. Re-add each step in the commit that lands its mechanism

package.json: test:coverage / test:integration / test:e2e intentionally NOT wired - an echo stub exiting 0 is laundering (lesson declared-method-needs-mechanism). qa-verify battery kept verbatim so it reports them MISSING rather than hiding the gap

quality/visual-parity.config.json omitted and scripts/check-parity-suite + parity-block-loop + lib/parity-capture not installed: no pixel-fidelity requirement in this project; check-visual-fidelity correctly renders NOT-EARNED
Claude Code PostToolUse ESLint hook installed in .claude/settings.json (user-approved after security-classifier block)
openspec init skipped: repo already carries 10 capability specs + capability-map.yaml + config.yaml (no openspec/project.md by design)
ci.yml: fetch-depth 0 so check-trajectory Slice trailer grep sees full history on GitHub Actions

improve-PD-3 (Refs: PD-3): check-trajectory.mjs now reads .project-factory/retrofit.json and renders RETROFITTED/NOT-EARNED instead of PASS; the 10 back-stamped review-findings.json were deleted as forged evidence (one, add-form-input, contradicted its own review doc). Executed red->green proof: tests/check-trajectory-retrofit.test.mjs (3/7 -> 7/7). Narrowing: non-zero exit only under --release so the pre-commit hook stays passable; the Result: string reads NOT-EARNED in both modes, which is what gate-status and qa-verify classify on.
NOTE for future reseals: --init-lock REPLACES the adaptations array, it does not merge. A prior session's reseal silently dropped 8 notes. Always read factory-lock.json first and re-pass every note.

improve-PD-9 (Refs: PD-9): check-traceability.mjs @trace parser now shares ID_PATTERN with idsIn, so categorized ids (FR-CALC-01) register. Executed red->green: tests/check-traceability-trace-ids.test.mjs 4/6 -> 6/6; real repo test-trace warnings 34 -> 33 when one annotation is added (delta was 0 before). Gate fixtures live under tests/.gate-fixtures/ because PATHS.testDirs includes tests/ and a fixture annotation was silently crediting the real FR-CALC-01.

improve-PD-7 (Refs: PD-7): ci.yml runs gate-status.mjs in its own job (set -o pipefail is load-bearing: bash -e without pipefail lets tee mask a non-zero exit, measured 0 vs 1) and executes the improve-PD-3/PD-9 red->green proofs on every run. The gate-status job is EXPECTED red until G6; keep it out of required checks rather than adding continue-on-error. Note: .github/workflows/*.yml ARE gate-bearing and in the lock.

Reseal after merge: .github/workflows/sync-homework-pr.yml drifted in commit 1d495d2 (comment-only: mentor PR #50 -> #56). It is a homework-branch sync workflow, NOT a quality gate, but factory-lock captures every .github/workflows/*.yml. Resealed rather than reverted; the drift is comment-only and visible in git history. OPEN DEFECT (PD-11, unfiled): the lock's gate-bearing scope over-captures non-gate workflows, so routine edits to them look like tampering and pressure a reseal — which is exactly the tamper-evidence-erasing action the lock exists to prevent. Narrow the scope, or move non-gate workflows out of it.

improve-PD-12 (Refs: PD-12): check-traceability's @trace parser is ANCHORED to the start of a comment. Unanchored it credited any mention: a `// No @trace FR-EXPORT-02: this test cannot prove it` disclaimer re-credited four refuted requirements, and a fixture under tests/ credited the real FR-CALC-01. Executed red->green: tests/check-traceability-anchor.test.mjs 2/4 -> 4/4; traced count held at 20/34; pd-3 7/7 and pd-9 6/6 unchanged. The anchor test is wired into CI Gate self-tests.

improve-PD-1 (Refs: PD-1): check-traceability.mjs and check-acceptance-methods.mjs both learn that a `| dropped |` status is non-MVP, like `| Future |`. FR-NACE-06 and FR-INPUT-03 (dropped negative requirements) leave the MVP set in both. Executed red->green: tests/check-dropped-status.test.mjs 2/5 -> 5/5; real repo MVP FRs 38 -> 36, warnings 53 -> 49. Two gate-bearing files drift together under one Refs: PD-1 commit.

improve-PD-10 (Refs: PD-10): check-trajectory Slice: trailer counts only if the commit carries a REAL trailer AND touched src/app/lib/db/components/tests. A docs-only commit (5bcbfe9 claimed 10 slices, 0 src files) no longer counts. Executed red->green: tests/check-slice-trailer.test.mjs (real git-repo fixtures) 1/2 -> 2/2.

improve-PD-8 (Refs: PD-8): check-trajectory trusts clean:true review evidence only from a real review-gate output (generatedBy: review-gate + populated dimensions). A bare {clean:true} or generatedBy:retrofit is an unverified-stamp, fails under --release. Executed red->green: tests/check-review-evidence.test.mjs 3/6 -> 6/6.

improve-PD-14-15 (Refs: PD-14, PD-15): review-gate.js agent types now project-factory:-prefixed (bare names did not resolve, lost 2 dimensions in S5 round 1); slice dependency audit scoped to slice-changed deps so a pre-existing transitive next/postcss advisory no longer blocks clean:true on every slice; verifier lenses configurable (default 1 for slice reviews, both via args.thorough or global). node --check passes.

Correction to improve-PD-14-15: reverted the single-lens 'lighter review' change. The exploitability lens is load-bearing — it contests real-but-unreachable footguns; dropping it made round-3 confirm 16 minor footguns that 2 lenses would have contested. PD-14 (agentType prefixes) and PD-15 (scoped dependency audit) are KEPT.

improve-PD-14 (extended): prefixed bare agent-only names in the remaining 5 workflows (spec-pipeline spec-writer, eval-suite + trajectory-eval eval-judge, vision-verify vision-judge, uat-triage bug-triage-analyst) with project-factory:. All 6 workflows now resolve their specialist agents.

improve-PD-16 (Refs: PD-16): review-gate clean = 'no confirmed DEFECTS', excluding positive 'Clean —'/'Coverage summary' notes. Before, thorough security reviews (which emit clean-aspect notes) could never reach clean:true, blocking every earned slice from archiving under PD-8.

improve-PD-16 (extended): isPositiveNote also excludes 'Verified —' notes (reviewers emit them like 'Clean —' to confirm a fix is sound). Without it a positive 'Verified — deep-freeze fix present' counted as a confirmed defect.

improve-PD-17 (Refs: PD-17): review-gate reviewers ignore **/review-findings.json — the gate's own evidence artifact, written at the end of each run, always lags HEAD by one run and produced a self-referential 'stale artifact' finding every pass.

improve-PD-16 (hardened): isPositiveNote requires a separator (em-dash or colon) after Clean/Verified/Coverage summary, so a real defect that merely starts with the word ('Verified email requirement not enforced') stays in the defect set. The review-gate itself flagged the title-prefix-only version as a major hole.
